payload.title: Custom, reorderable board columns
payload.description:
Turn board columns from a hardcoded global status enum into per-workspace, user-defined, reorderable columns: add/rename/recolor/reorder/delete columns. Reorderable columns is the easy 20% of this; the bulk is converting the enum into synced workspace data without regressing the semantics currently wired to the literal status 'done'.

Key design points:
- Each column carries a semantic KIND (open | done | cancelled) so epic progress, confetti, open-work detection, and OPEN_STATUSES keep working off the flag, not the string 'done'.
- Status validation becomes DYNAMIC against the workspace's column set (today it's a frozen enum validated in 3 places) — must tolerate events arriving from other clients referencing a column this replica hasn't seen yet.
- Column config (id, label, color, kind, order) is persisted on the workspace via a new event kind and synced like everything else; existing boards seed from today's six statuses.
- Phased AFTER persistent ticket ordering.
